content.description:
A widget that displays all relationships for the currently selected item. Shows forward relationships (type, tags, parent, etc.) and inverse relationships (instances, children, linked from, etc.) using the [related-items-lib](item://f5f8535c-8db9-4bff-a810-28174b77235c) indexes.

## Architecture

- **Type**: `related-items-widget` — instances are placed in the viewport like any item
- **View**: [system:related-items-view](item://34237dbd-ce58-46f7-ab19-2213c4ae0a51) — renders the widget, watches `viewport:selection-changed` to reactively update
- **Data**: [related-items-lib](item://f5f8535c-8db9-4bff-a810-28174b77235c) provides `getRelated(itemId, api)` returning all forward and inverse relationships

The view watches selection changes via a declarative watch on `e0e00000-0003-0001-0000-000000000000` and calls `api.rerenderByType()` to refresh all widget instances. It skips re-rendering when the widget itself is selected.

## Relationship Groups

**Forward** (read from the item): Type, Tags, Parent, Extends, For Type, Preferred View, Attachments, Links To

**Inverse** (from indexes): Instances, Tagged With, Children, Subtypes, Contained In, Linked From, Views For, Preferred By

Only non-empty groups are shown. Groups with >10 items are truncated.

## Changes Made Alongside This Widget

### Kernel: Unified Event Dispatch

`setupDeclarativeWatches` was split into two paths — one for `ITEM_EVENT` and one for `SYSTEM_EVENT`. The widget needed viewport events (which are neither), exposing a design issue: `evaluateWatchFilter` returned `false` when no item was present, even for watches with no filters. This blocked filterless watches (like viewport events) from ever matching.

**Fix**: Single `EVENT_DEFINITION` subscription receives all events. `evaluateWatchFilter` now returns `true` when no filters are specified. `dispatchToSystemWatchers` and `findSystemEventHandlers` removed (~54 lines). System error fallback folded into the unified path.

### selection-manager: Event GUID Fix

Was using `api.EVENT_IDS.VIEWPORT_SELECTION_CHANGED` which was `undefined` (removed from kernel EVENT_IDS as "defined by userland"). Every `select()` call emitted an event with `type: undefined` — a silent no-op. Fixed by using a local GUID constant.

### related-items-lib: globalThis State

Index Maps and the `built` flag moved from module scope to `globalThis.__relatedItemsLib__` so they survive `moduleSystem.clearCache()`. Event handlers call `ensureBuilt(api)` instead of bailing when `!built`.

## Known Issues

### Stale indexes after code item import

After importing code items, the widget sometimes gets stuck showing stale or empty data despite the `globalThis` fix and `ensureBuilt` recovery. The root cause is not yet fully understood — it may involve `clearCache()` interacting with the declarative watch dispatch (the lib's own event handler runs on a module instance that is about to be discarded), the `selection-manager` losing its `selectedItemId` (also module-scoped state that doesn't survive cache clears), or a rendering system timing issue where the widget re-renders before the new module code is loaded.

**Workaround**: Refresh the page (F5).

**Partially addressed**: `selection-manager` state moved to `globalThis` (same pattern as related-items-lib), so `selectedItemId` and `api` survive `clearCache()`. This fixes the widget getting stuck after item deletion. Remaining staleness after code item import may still occur due to other factors:
- Investigate whether `clearCache()` should be more targeted (invalidate only the changed module, not all modules)
- Add a `system:module-cache-cleared` event that stateful libraries can watch to rebuild
- Trace the exact sequence during import to identify the specific failure point

## See Also

- [Architecture Overview](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000003)
- [Module Cache Gotcha](item://c0c0c0c0-0016-0000-0000-000000000000)
